Defendant'S Domco Products Texas, Inc.'S. Ex Parte Application For An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Produce Floor Tile For Destructive Testing
Off Calendar Feb-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party DOMCO PRODUCTS TEXAS INC.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Alvin W. Kinkel, et al.

RULING:
Cousel presents argument off the record. On the record, the Court grants Defendant DOMCO PRODUCTS TEXAS INC.'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER FOR DESTRUCTIVE TESTING OF FLOOR TILES. The Court orders that the tiles which are now in the possession of an expert retained by Domco remain in that expert's possession and no destructive testing occur until noon of February 11, 2011. The plaintiff may send a person over to where the tiles are to retrieve the box as well as the 14 tiles and to determine if the tiles are in fact are all identical. If there is a good faith basis to believe that all the tiles are not identical, then there shall be no testing until the dispute has been completely resolved. If there is no dispute, then counsel for Domco, probably through the expert retained by Domco, may send the two tiles off to a testing lab on the East Coast. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Off Calendar Jan-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Emily K. Ayers, Esq., of POND NORTH (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party Sears, Roebuck and Co.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Ernest Stone

RULING:
DEFENDANT SEARS, ROEBUCK AND CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he purchased asbestos-containing auto parts from defendant. Defendant's separate statement fails to show that any of the auto parts that plaintiff purchased from defendant did not contain asbestos other than a) Bendix, Raybestos and Borg-Warner brakes; and brakes purchased in 1985 or later, b) Bendix, Raybestos and Borg-Warner clutch parts and automotive gaskets, and c) Victor and Fel-Pro automotive gaskets from 1950-92. Plaintiff represents that he dismissed his claims for false representation and punitive damages, the sole grounds for the alternate motion for summary adjudication, although the court sees no dismissal on Lexis. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
